title: Unbounded recursion on deeply nested blocks
description: TRIGGER: a Sieve script with O(N) nested if/block structures (each { opens a block). read_stmt calls read_block which calls read_stmt — mutual recursion with no depth guard. A crafted 10,000-deep script exhausts the default Rust stack and panics with a stack overflow instead of returning a clean ParseError. BAD BEHAVIOR: process panics rather than returning Err(ParseError). Fix: add a depth counter threaded through read_stmt/read_block and return ParseError when it exceeds a reasonable limit (e.g. 200).
